summary: Operates, validates, and maintains digital‑twin simulations that model real‑world processes, enabling predictive analysis, scenario testing, and safe experimentation within the CI‑OS.
responsibilities: Configure, run, and monitor digital‑twin simulations for processes, workflows, and equipment
Validate simulation accuracy by comparing digital outputs with real‑world performance data
Collaborate with Process Engineers and Data Analysts to design simulation scenarios
Support predictive modeling for throughput, reliability, quality, and risk assessments
Document simulation parameters, assumptions, and outcomes for auditability
Identify discrepancies between digital and physical systems and recommend corrections
Maintain simulation models, ensuring they reflect current processes and configurations
Support training scenarios and virtual gemba sessions for CI‑OS roles
Integrate simulation outputs into dashboards, workflows, and improvement pipelines
Ensure simulations comply with governance, safety, and data integrity standards
authorities: Flag discrepancies between digital and physical systems
Recommend process or system changes based on simulation insights
Escalate high‑risk simulation findings to leadership
decisionRights: Determination of simulation readiness for training use
kaizenCapabilities: Identification of improvement opportunities through scenario testing
Support for waste elimination via virtual experimentation
cqiCapabilities: Integration of simulation metrics into CQI dashboards

constraints: Models must remain synchronized with real‑world processes
